---
title: "AI & Environmental Sustainability"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} · **Governance, Ethics & Equity** track · ~1 hour, self-paced
:::

[← Back to the Governance, Ethics & Equity track](governance-ethics.qmd){.track-back}

*AI has a physical footprint — understand it and make proportionate choices.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Describe the energy and water cost of AI compute
- Right-size model use to the task
- Weigh benefit against environmental cost in your work

## Overview

AI runs on data centers that consume electricity and water at scale. The footprint is real and growing, and it's an environmental-health issue squarely in DSPH's wing. Awareness doesn't mean abstention — it means proportion.

Right-sizing is the practical lever: use the smallest model that does the job, avoid wasteful re-runs, and reserve heavy compute for tasks that warrant it.

## Practice activities

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 1 · Novice — Right-size a task</summary>
```

**Time** ~12 min · **Tools** ChatGPT Edu (model picker)

**Goal.** Use the smallest model that does the job.

**Setup.** A simple task you’d normally send to a large model.

**Steps.**

1. Run it on a smaller/faster model and judge whether quality held.

2. Run it on the large model and compare.

3. Note where the smaller model is good enough.

**Expected result.** A sense of where “smaller is fine.”

**Check your work.** Did the big model actually add value here, or just cost more energy and money?

**Common pitfalls.** Defaulting to the biggest model for everything is wasteful.

**Stretch (optional).** Estimate how often your typical tasks really need the largest model.

```{=html}
</details>
```

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 2 · Novice — Connect AI’s footprint to environmental health</summary>
```

**Time** ~13 min · **Tools** a reading + ChatGPT Edu

**Goal.** Frame AI’s resource use as a DSPH-relevant issue.

**Setup.** Read one source on data-center energy or water use (in your DSPH Readings folder).

**Steps.**

1. Note the key figures (energy, water).

2. Draft a framing:

   > Summarize the environmental-health and equity concerns of data-center siting in two sentences for a public audience.

3. Verify the figures against the reading.

**Expected result.** A short, accurate framing connecting AI to environmental health.

**Check your work.** Confirm the numbers — don’t repeat an AI-stated figure unchecked.

**Common pitfalls.** This is a real tradeoff, not a reason to abstain; aim for proportion.

**Stretch (optional).** Draft a one-line “responsible use” norm for your team.

```{=html}
</details>
```


## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "The main environmental costs of AI compute are…", "options": ["Electricity and water for data centers", "Paper and ink", "Bandwidth only", "None"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Data centers draw significant power and water."}, {"q": "The practical lever for individuals is…", "options": ["Right-sizing model use to the task", "Never using AI", "Using the biggest model always", "Ignoring it"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Use the smallest model that does the job."}, {"q": "Why is this a DSPH issue?", "options": ["It's an environmental-health and equity concern", "It isn't", "Only IT cares", "It's purely financial"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Siting and resource use affect communities."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

From the AI readings library (ask the facilitator for access):

- **Data Centers and Water Consumption (EESI)** — the water footprint of AI infrastructure.
- **Are Data Centers Bad for the Environment? (Deseret News)** — a balanced look at the impacts.
- **AI Data Centers Create Fury From Mexico to Ireland (NYT)** — community and equity dimensions.

## Useful resources

- [ML CO2 Impact](https://mlco2.github.io/impact/) — estimate the carbon of compute.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
No sensitive data needed here; this topic is about awareness and proportionate use. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
